data.title: Prairie Operating Co. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Prairie Operating Co., a key player in the energy sector focusing on crude oil, natural gas, and NGL production, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational advancements, underscoring the company's strategic initiatives and robust performance in a competitive market.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $77.7 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting the company's growth in crude oil, natural gas, and NGL sales.
  • Operating Income: $27.0 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, indicating a strong operational performance compared to a loss in the previous year.
  • Net Income: $1.3 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, showing a significant improvement from a net loss in the prior year.
  • Net Loss Attributable to Prairie Operating Co. Common Stockholders: $(22.5) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to preferred stock dividends and remeasurement.
  • Basic and Diluted Loss Per Share: $(0.44) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting the impact of preferred stock dividends and remeasurement on common stockholders.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue from crude oil, natural gas, and NGL sales. Crude oil sales were the largest contributor, followed by natural gas and NGL sales. This indicates a diversified revenue stream within the energy sector.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations are strategically located in the oil-rich region of rural Weld County, Colorado, within the Denver–Julesburg Basin. This location is significant for its potential in oil and gas production.
  • Sales Units: The company has significant production volumes with crude oil, natural gas, and NGLs being the primary products. The company utilizes commodity derivative instruments to manage price volatility, indicating a proactive approach to stabilizing revenue from these sales.
  • New Production Launches: The company completed the Bayswater Acquisition and the Edge Acquisition, which expanded its operational capacity. These acquisitions included additional oil and natural gas properties, enhancing the company's production capabilities.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue growing its business through accretive acquisitions, focusing on assets with producing reserves and opportunities for undeveloped bolt-on acreage. This strategy aims to leverage high rate-of-return inventory of drilling locations and strong well-level economics.
  • Operational Strategy: The company operates in one business segment focused on the acquisition, development, and production of crude oil, natural gas, and NGLs. This streamlined focus allows for concentrated efforts in optimizing production and operational efficiency.

SEC Filing: Prairie Operating Co. [ PROP ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: MAUI LAND & PINEAPPLE CO INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Maui Land & Pineapple Co Inc, a company primarily engaged in land development, leasing, and resort amenities in Maui, Hawaii, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial performance and business operations, indicating a positive turnaround for the company.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Operating Revenues: $4.5 million, increased from $3.0 million in the same quarter last year, indicating a recovery in leasing and land development activities.
  • Operating Loss: $0.36 million, improved from a loss of $2.2 million in the same quarter last year, reflecting better cost management and increased revenues.
  • Net Income: $0.24 million, compared to a net loss of $2.2 million in the same quarter last year, driven by increased revenues and a significant pension-related income.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Common Share - Basic: $0.01, compared to a loss of $0.11 per share in the same quarter last year, reflecting improved profitability.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Common Share - Diluted: $0.01, compared to a loss of $0.11 per share in the same quarter last year, indicating a positive turnaround in earnings.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported operating revenues from three main segments: Land Development and Sales, Leasing, and Resort Amenities. The Leasing segment showed significant growth, with revenues increasing from $7.1 million in 2024 to $9.9 million in 2025, driven by commercial, industrial, and agricultural leases.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations are primarily based in Maui, Hawaii, with significant landholdings and commercial properties. The report highlights efforts to maximize the productivity of these assets, particularly in West Maui and Upcountry Maui.
  • Sales Units: The company has been actively involved in land sales, with three non-strategic parcel sales completed in the first nine months of 2025. Additionally, the Honokeana Homes project contributed to land development revenues.
  • New Product Launches: The company initiated the planting of agave in the second quarter of 2025, marking the start of a new venture aimed at producing agave-based products. This aligns with their strategy to create sustainable agricultural opportunities.
  • New Production Launches: The company is involved in the Honokeana Homes project, which includes leasing land to the State of Hawaii for temporary housing to support wildfire victims. This project involves significant horizontal improvements funded by the state.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue enhancing its commercial properties to attract top-tier tenants and expects cash flow from these properties to increase. They are also progressing with strategic plans for their landholdings to meet community needs, including housing and agriculture.

SEC Filing: MAUI LAND & PINEAPPLE CO INC [ MLP ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Inhibikase Therapeutics, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Inhibikase Therapeutics, Inc., a clinical-stage pharmaceutical company focused on developing therapeutics for the treatment of Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension (PAH) and other diseases, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2023. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and key business developments, reflecting significant progress in its lead product candidate, IKT-001, and strategic operational shifts.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Loss: $(11.93) million, an increase of 106.5% compared to the prior period, primarily due to higher research and development expenses and selling, general and administrative expenses.
  • Loss from Operations: $(12.77) million, up by 119.1% from the prior period, driven by increased costs in PAH research and development and stock-based compensation.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $(0.13), compared to $(0.65) in the prior period, reflecting the impact of increased expenses on a per-share basis.
  • Interest Income: $0.84 million, a significant increase of 1,596.2% from the prior period, primarily due to interest earned on cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities.

Business Highlights

  • Product Development - IKT-001: Inhibikase Therapeutics is advancing its lead product candidate, IKT-001, a prodrug of imatinib mesylate, for the treatment of Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension (PAH). The company has completed non-human primate safety studies and a bioequivalence clinical trial in healthy volunteers, setting the stage for a Phase 2b study expected to initiate in Q4 2025.
  • Clinical Trials - IMPROVE-PAH: The upcoming Phase 2b study, IMPROVE-PAH, will be a multi-center, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study involving approximately 150 PAH patients. The primary endpoint is the change in pulmonary vascular resistance at Week 26, with secondary endpoints including 6-minute walk distance and WHO functional class.
  • Discontinued Program - Risvodetinib: The company has discontinued and outlicensed its risvodetinib program, previously targeting Parkinson's disease, to ABLi Therapeutics, Inc.
  • Operational Focus - PAH: The company is focusing its resources on the development of IKT-001 for PAH, with significant increases in research and development expenses allocated to this program.
  • Future Outlook - Regulatory Strategy: Inhibikase is engaging with the FDA regarding its Phase 3 strategy for PAH and plans to apply for Orphan Drug Designation for IKT-001 once required pre-clinical studies are complete.
  • Patent Protection: The company holds patent protection in the United States for IKT-001 until 2033, with potential extensions for certain methods of treatment using IKT-001 until 2044.
  • Market Opportunity - PAH: The global PAH market was valued at $7.66 billion in 2023, with a projected compound annual growth rate of 3.3% through 2034, highlighting a significant market opportunity for IKT-001.
  • Clinical Trial Supply and Research Organization Agreements: Inhibikase has entered into agreements with a clinical trial supply organization and a contract research organization to support the IMPROVE-PAH study, with estimated total remaining contract costs of approximately $6.3 million and $19.0 million, respectively.
  • Regulatory Pathway - 505(b)(2): The FDA has confirmed that the 505(b)(2) pathway is appropriate for the approval of IKT-001, potentially granting New Molecular Entity status and market exclusivity upon approval.

SEC Filing: Inhibikase Therapeutics, Inc. [ IKT ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Farmers & Merchants Bancshares, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Farmers & Merchants Bancshares, Inc., a prominent player in the banking industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report showcases the company's robust financial performance and strategic business initiatives, reflecting its commitment to growth and stability in a competitive market.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $31.1 million, an increase of $3.0 million or 11.0% compared to the same period in 2024, driven by higher interest income on loans.
  • Total Interest Expense: $13.3 million, an increase of $600 thousand or 4.7% compared to the same period in 2024, due to a $33.3 million increase in the average balance of interest-bearing liabilities.
  • Net Interest Income: $17.8 million, up from $15.4 million for the same period of 2024, with a net yield on interest-earning assets rising to 2.98% from 2.67%.
  • Net Income: $4.1 million, compared to $3.4 million for the same period of 2024, reflecting improved financial performance.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $595 thousand, compared to $0 for the same period in 2024, indicating a more cautious approach to potential credit risks.
  • Noninterest Income: $1.5 million, an increase of $191 thousand or 14.4% from the same period in 2024, primarily due to gains on the settlement of a fair value hedge and increased mortgage banking income.
  • Noninterest Expense: $13.6 million, an increase of $1.3 million or 10.8% from the same period in 2024, largely due to higher staffing levels and increased Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation premiums.
  • Income Tax Expense: $1.1 million, an increase of $65 thousand from the same period in 2024, with a decrease in the effective tax rate to 20.6% from 22.5%.

Business Highlights

  • Loan Portfolio Growth: The company experienced a significant increase in its loan portfolio, with net loans rising by $39.5 million, or 6.8%, primarily due to increases in commercial real estate loans, construction loans, and commercial loans. This growth was attributed to the addition of new lending staff and stabilizing interest rates.
  • Credit Risk Management: The company has implemented policies and procedures to mitigate credit risk and maintain loan portfolio quality. These include underwriting standards, continuous monitoring, and reporting of asset quality, as well as the adequacy of the allowance for credit losses.
  • Watch List Loans: As of September 30, 2025, the company had $35.9 million of loans on a watch list, indicating potential financial difficulties for borrowers. This is a significant increase from $8.1 million at the end of 2024, highlighting a focus on managing credit risk.
  • Investment Securities: Investments in debt securities decreased by $3.9 million, or 2.7%, with 85% of the investment portfolio classified as available for sale. The company manages its investment portfolio to achieve liquidity, manage interest rate sensitivity, and meet earnings objectives.
  • Other Real Estate Owned (OREO): The company reported an increase in OREO, with properties including a strip shopping center, an apartment building, and a land parcel. These properties are actively being marketed for sale.
  • Deposit Trends: Total deposits decreased by $24.7 million, or 3.3%, primarily due to a reduction in brokered CDs and interest-bearing checking accounts. However, there were increases in CDARS deposits, demand deposits, and money market accounts.
  • Liquidity Management: The company maintains a strong liquidity position through short-term investments, loan repayments, and income from earning assets. It has secured lines of credit with the Federal Home Loan Bank and the Federal Reserve Bank, ensuring adequate liquidity to meet financial obligations.
  • Borrowings: The company repaid a $10.0 million Merger Loan using proceeds from the issuance of $12.5 million in Subordinated Notes. These notes are intended to qualify as Tier 2 capital for regulatory purposes.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company reported an increase in noninterest income by 14.4% and noninterest expense by 10.8% for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting higher staffing levels and increased operational costs.
  • Future Outlook: Management believes that the company has adequate liquidity sources to meet anticipated needs over the next 12 months and is not aware of any trends or events likely to impact its ability to maintain satisfactory liquidity levels.

SEC Filing: Farmers & Merchants Bancshares, Inc. [ FMFG ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Fitch Downgrades SK Mohawk to 'C'
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-New York-14 November 2025:

Fitch has downgraded the Long-Term Issuer Default Ratings (IDRs) of SK Mohawk Holdings, SARL and Polar US Borrower, LLC (collectively, SI Group) to 'C' from 'CC'. Fitch has also downgraded the issue rating of the first-lien first-out revolver to 'CCC' with a Recovery Rating of 'RR1' from 'CCC+'/'RR1' and affirmed the first-lien second-out term loan at 'C'/'RR5'. The rating of the senior unsecured notes is affirmed at 'C'/'RR6'.

Key Rating Drivers

--

RATING SENSITIVITIES

--

Issuer Profile

SI Group (SK Mohawk Holdings, SARL) provides polymers, fuel, lubricant and industrial additives and chemical intermediates for use in various end markets, including plastics, fuels, tires, oilfield chemicals, food packaging and surfactants.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. MACROECONOMIC ASSUMPTIONS AND SECTOR FORECASTS Click here to access Fitch's latest quarterly Global Corporates Sector Forecasts Monitor data file which aggregates key data points used in our credit analysis. Fitch's macroeconomic forecasts, commodity price assumptions, default rate forecasts, sector key performance indicators and sector-level forecasts are among the data items included. ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

data.title: Mars Sour firms as Brent/WTI spread widens
data.description:
Coastal grade Mars Sour firmed on Friday, dealers said, as the discount between Brent and U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude widened for a fifth straight session.

India's Bharat Petroleum Corp <BPCL.NS>bought one million barrels of U.S. Mars crude from Shell SHEL.L, sources said, as a part of 3.7 million barrels of crude oil spot purchases from West Africa, United States and the Middle East this week to replace oil from Russia.

Mars gained 35 cents, while WTI Midland eased 5 cents.

The spread between Brent and WTI widened to minus $4.45 earlier in the session. A spread larger than minus $4 typically encourages exports.

State-owned QatarEnergy has raised the term premium for al-Shaheen crude oil loading in January as demand rose from Indian refiners seeking alternatives to replace Russian oil, four trade sources said on Friday.

On the supply side, Baker Hughes said oil rigs rose by three to 417 this week, their highest since October 24.

In refining news, U.S. oil refiners are expected to have about 790,000 barrels per day of capacity offline in the week ending November 14, increasing available refining capacity by 176,000 bpd, research company IIR Energy said on Friday.

  • Light Louisiana Sweet for December delivery eased by 30 cents to a midpoint of a $1.00 premium and was seen bid and offered between a 80-cent and $1.20 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • Mars Sour firmed to a midpoint of a 90-cent discount and was seen bid and offered between a $1.10 and 70-cent a barrel discount to U.S. crude futures

    ​

  • WTI Midland eased at a midpoint of a 45-cent premium and was seen bid and offered between a 25-cent and 65-cent a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

    ​

  • West Texas Sour firmed to a midpoint of a 80-cent discount and was seen bid and offered between a $1.00 and 60-cent a barrel discount to U.S. crude futures ​

  • WTI at East Houston, also known as MEH, traded between a 60-cent and $1.00 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • ICE Brent January futures rose $1.38 to settle at $64.39 a barrel on Friday​

  • WTI December crude futures rose $1.4 to settle at $60.09 a barrel on Friday​

  • The Brent/WTI spread widened 7 cents to last trade at minus $4.44, after hitting a high of minus $4.30 and a low of minus $4.45

data.title: Sidus Space Q3 revenue falls, net loss widens
data.description:
Overview

  • Sidus Space Q3 2025 revenue falls 31% yr/yr due to strategic pivot

  • Net loss widens to $6.0 mln from $3.9 mln in Q3 2024

  • Company raises capital to fund technology initiatives across multiple domains

Outlook

  • Company focuses on aligning spend with near-term revenue milestones

  • Sidus Space aims to expand global partnerships and accelerate commercialization

  • Company identifies operational efficiencies to reduce SG&A expenses

Result Drivers

  • STRATEGIC PIVOT - Revenue decreased 31% due to shift from legacy services to new commercial models

  • COST INCREASES - Higher costs driven by increased satellite depreciation and material and labor expenses

  • TECHNOLOGY EXPANSION - Capital raised to fund technology initiatives across multiple domains

Key Details

Metric

Q3 Revenue

Beat/Miss

Actual

$1.30 mln

Consensus Estimate

Analyst Coverage

  • The one available analyst rating on the shares is "buy"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the aerospace & defense peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Sidus Space Inc is $10.00, about 91.8% above its November 13 closing price of $0.82

Press Release:

data.title: Rektron Group Provides Operation Update
data.description:
DL Hudson Applies for Administration to Facilitate Strategic Restructuring

VANCOUVER, BC / ACCESS Newswire / November 14, 2025 / Rektron Group Inc. (CSE:REK.U)(FRA:F75) ("Rektron" or the "Company"), a global commodity trading group with subsidiaries that specialise in trading energy, metals, and energy transition commodities, provides an update on its subsidiary, DL Hudson Ltd ("DLH"), that is currently going through strategic restructuring.

DLH has received strong indications of interest from several potential investors. These parties have expressed confidence in the company's long-term prospects and see meaningful opportunities to support its next phase of growth. The planned approach is expected to bring not only fresh capital, but also access to broader markets, strategic partnerships, and operational expertise. Together, these benefits would help reinforce DLH's financial position, enhance its competitive capabilities, and create a more robust platform to drive sustainable performance going forward.

DLH is currently undergoing a self-determined application for administration to facilitate its restructuring within the business for the potential investors as per appropriate legal advice in light of the legal proceedings in the United Kingdom arising from a litigation with a distressed lender. For the benefit of its suppliers, counterparties, creditors and investors DLH has taken this measure to enable a controlled and efficient transition into its next stage of growth.

DLH continues to trade as normal, with all customers, suppliers and operational commitments being maintained. Rektron fully supports this forward-looking step as part of its wider strategy to enhance the group's financial resilience, optimise capital allocation and expand its international reach.

Further updates will be provided as the transition progresses and the business combination is finalised.

About Rektron Group Inc.

Rektron Group Inc. (CSE:REK.U)(FRA:F75), through its subsidiaries, is a global physical commodities trading and infrastructure company. Focused on delivering sustainable supply chain operations, across energy and metals commodity markets.

The Company's vertically integrated model combines physical commodity trading, logistics, structured financing, and risk management under one platform, enabling efficient and secure global trade. Through its core divisions, the Company is positioned at the intersection of industrial growth and the global energy transition.

Rektron is committed to driving long-term value through innovation, responsible resource management, and a strategic focus on circular economy principles and low-carbon solutions.

Please visit www.rektrongroup.com for further information.

Forward-Looking Statements

data.title: Real estate firm Maui Land & Pineapple's Q3 operating revenues rise
data.description:
Overview

  • Maui Land & Pineapple Q3 operating revenues up 83.1% yr/yr, driven by leasing growth

  • Company's recurring leasing revenue increased 39% year-to-date compared to 2024

  • Net loss increased due to $6.9 mln pension expense, impacting financial results

Outlook

  • Company anticipates continued increases in recurring net operating income as occupancy stabilizes

  • Company expects to reduce share-based compensation expenses in future periods

Result Drivers

  • LEASING REVENUE - Co reports 39% increase in recurring leasing revenue year-to-date due to new tenants and improved occupancy

  • LAND DEVELOPMENT - Sale of three land parcels contributed to improved net operating income in land development segment

  • AGRICULTURE VENTURE - Co launched new agave agriculture venture, planting 15,000 plants to develop value-added products

data.title: CYCU: Q3 2025 revenue fell 13.9% with a $3.1M net loss; Nasdaq compliance was regained post-reverse split
data.description:

data.title: Beam Global Files 8K - Operations And Financial Condition >BEEM
data.description:
Beam Global (BEEM) filed a Form 8K - Operations and Financial Condition - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on November 14, 2025.

On November 14, 2025 Beam Global (the "Company") issued a press release announcing financial results for its quarter ending September 30, 2025. A copy of the press release is furnished as Exhibit 99.1 to this Current Report on Form 8-K and is incorporated herein by reference.

The information furnished in this Form 8-K and the press release attached as Exhibit 99.1 shall not be deemed "filed" for purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, or otherwise subject to the liabilities of that section or Sections 11 and 12(a)(2) of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended. The information contained in this Item 2.02 and in the press release attached as Exhibit 99.1 shall not be incorporated by reference into any filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission made by the Company, whether made before or after the date hereof, regardless of any general incorporation language in such filing.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1398805/000143774925035217/beem20250917_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1398805/000143774925035217/0001437749-25-035217-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: IKT: Phase 2b PAH trial for IKT-001 set for Q4 2025 amid rising expenses and net loss
data.description:
Advanced IKT-001 toward a Phase 2b PAH trial set for Q4 2025, with increased R&D and SG&A expenses driving a higher net loss year-over-year. Cash reserves declined to $77.3 million, and significant non-cash charges were recorded from the CorHepta acquisition.

data.title: VRCA: Revenue surged in Q3 2025, but liquidity concerns persist without additional funding
data.description:
Q3 2025 saw a sharp revenue increase to $14.3M, driven by product sales and a $10M milestone from Torii, with net loss narrowing to $0.3M. Cash of $21.1M is expected to last into late Q4 2025, but substantial doubt remains about ongoing viability without new funding.
